retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The discrepancy of the crown margin adjoined to the pontic was increased by the sintering shrinkage of the bulky pontic in the case of 3-unit and 4-unit frameworks. Therefore, we must beware of the distortion of zirconia-based FDPs with long span units when using partially-sintered blocks or green blocks.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: The implant placement should be restoration driven to fulfill the patient’s goals. The prosthesis should be designed first, similar to the architect designing a building before making the foundation. Only after the prosthesis is created can the abutments, implant bodies, and available bone requirements be determined to support the specifically predetermined restoration.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Preparation -- Selection of Crown
retrieved_docs.content: The operator can select the crown using different methods: Measuring the mesiodistal dimension of the tooth before tooth preparation Trial and error method Crown selection after tooth preparation

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Indications for the use of stainless steel crowns: Following pulp therapy Multisurface caries restorations Patients at high risk of caries Deciduous teeth with developmental defects, such as amelogenesis imperfecta, dentinogenesis imperfecta, enamel hypoplasia Teeth with extensive wear Where it is likely that the restoration may fail, eg, the proximal box formed is extended beyond the anatomic line angles. Fractured teeth Abutment for space maintainer [6] Patients who are unlikely to attend follow up appointments [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: People are living longer on average. This fact, combined with an existing population of patients with minor and major dental problems, guarantees the future of implant dentistry for several generations of dentists. Dental implants are increasingly used to replace single teeth, especially in the posterior regions of the mouth. Rather than removing sound tooth structure and crowning two or more teeth, increasing the risk of decay, endodontic therapy, and splinting teeth together with pontics, which may have the potential to decrease oral hygiene ability and increase plaque retention, a dental implant may replace the single tooth.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: Place orthodontic separators between the contact point of the primary molar for 4 or 5 days to create room for the crown. Select the smallest-sized crown covering all the cusps, approaching the contact points, and giving a 'spring back' effect. Adjustments of the crown are to be made using band-forming pliers if necessary. Cementation of the crown with glass ionomer cement. The tooth must be maintained in occlusion until the cement sets, followed by excess removal using floss. [25] [26] The Hall technique should be chosen as an alternative when the traditional approach with crown preparation is implausible. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Buccal/Lingual Reduction
retrieved_docs.content: Reducing the buccal and lingual surfaces is the third step in the preparation. The reduction of the buccal and lingual mucosa is optional. Some authors suggested preparing the buccal and lingual walls to create a gingivally inclined long bevel to make crown placement easier. On the other hand, others advocated for none to minimal (0.5 to 1 mm) preparation on the vestibular and lingual aspect of the crown, unless pronounced enamel convexity should only be reduced by a small amount. [12] Duggal and Curzon recommended trying the selected crown before lingual and buccal reduction. [13] Lastly, the operator should round all line and point angles. Occlusal-lingual and occlusal-buccal angles are beveled at 45 degrees. Bur is held parallel to the tooth’s long axis, and one should carefully blend the surfaces. Remove any remaining caries and perform appropriate pulp therapy if pulp involvement is present. The rubber dam should be left in place until cementing the crown. If it interferes with the seating and adaptation of the crown, it can be removed after tooth preparation. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Cementation
retrieved_docs.content: A luting cement should be used to secure the crown. Once the crown is seated on the prepared tooth, it should be kept under pressure while the cement hardens. Manufacturers recommend glass ionomer, zinc polycarboxylate, and zinc phosphate cement but fluoride-leaching cement has additional advantages. Due to the clinical advantages, RMGIC is the preferred choice. [22] However, research suggests the type of cement used has little effect on retention, with the essential retentive components derived from proper crown contouring and crimping. Operators must pay deliberate attention to the removal of excess cement. This is normally accomplished by running a pointed instrument around the cemented crown's margins and sliding knotted dental floss buccolingually through the contact areas after the cement setting. Excess cement has been shown to affect gingival health. [8] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Organized dentistry has finally accepted implant dentistry. The current trend to expand the use of implant dentistry will continue until every restorative practice uses this modality for abutment support of both fixed and removable prostheses on a regular basis as the primary option for all tooth replacements.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Fluorosis -- Treatment Planning
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment planning is an individualized patient-centered process focusing on prevention and minimally invasive treatment modalities where possible. [26] Once the signs of dental fluorosis are evident in dentition clinically, it is too late for prevention strategies to be effective. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Retrognathia -- Treatment Planning
retrieved_docs.content: A patient who has retrognathia from mandibular AP hypoplasia will enter the treatment planning phase of their case during the first visit with the orthodontist. The orthodontist because will plan treatment years into the future and make movement and teeth extraction decisions based on skeletal growth and facial characteristics. Once the patient is ready for surgical correction, they will be referred to an oral and maxillofacial surgeon. A full series of photos and dental models are generally taken along with radiographs and a full workup completed and discussed with the treatment team, orthodontist, and patient. At this point, the surgical options are presented to the patient, and a surgical course decided upon. The standard workup and planning involving all proposed surgeries be completed on dental models and precise movements documented. Acrylic splints are then fabricated for use in the stabilization of the teeth and bones for rigid internal fixation during surgery. More common over the last decade is virtual surgical planning, where a computed tomography (CT) scan and patient models are digitally merged, and the operation is virtually planned within 0.01 mm; final surgical splints are custom printed on a 3D printer. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Restoration of Bruxism/Hypoplastic Teeth
retrieved_docs.content: Greater occlusal wear results from bruxism and hypoplastic disease, causing decreased vertical height. In this case, occlusion can be improved by adding a solder layer from the crown's impression surface to prevent or reduce occlusal reduction (Croll technique). The rest of the tooth preparation and crown adaptation is standard. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: 6. Indications: Fixed prosthesis: crown, bridges, implant Maxillofacial prosthodontics Registering occlusion Polyether Polyether has become popular because it only requires a stock tray and a single mix. 1. Mode of supply: Available as light, medium, and heavy body consistencies. 2. Composition: The base contains polyether, fillers, and plasticizers. The accelerator contains alkyl aromatic sulfonates, inert oils, and plasticizers. 3. Setting reaction: When base and accelerator are mixed in equal proportions, polyether rubber is formed. 4. Indications Fixed prosthodontics (crown and bridgework, particularly for multiple preparations) Implants Border molding in complete denture fabrication Indirect inlay and onlay impressions It can be used for corrective impressions as it can be added to previously set material. 5. Advantages:

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Teeth -- Indications -- Pre-prosthetic Extractions
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of some teeth can be considered if it contributes to a better-fitting patient prosthesis or if teeth are considered to have a poor enough prognosis that they will later on adversely affect the suitability of a prosthesis. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Fluorosis -- Treatment Planning
retrieved_docs.content: The primary concern for most patients affected by dental fluorosis is aesthetics. [24] Once patients start on the restorative cycle, they will require lifelong maintenance of their restorations. [32] For this reason, especially where there are minimal symptoms and functional deficits, treatment plans must be centered around the principles of minimally invasive dentistry when possible. [20] Severe cases of dental fluorosis may warrant more destructive therapeutic options, but patients must be fully aware of the risks. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: In dentistry, with the development of computer-aided design (CAD)/computeraided manufacturing (CAM) systems, high strength zirconia can be used for the fabrication of crowns, veneers, fixed partial dentures, posts and cores, implants, and implant abutments. In addition, different auxiliary components such as cutting burs and surgical drills, extra-coronal attachments, and orthodontic brackets manufactured using zirconia are available commercially. [7] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 961
retrieved_docs.content: labor and delivery. Recognizing these interrelated conditions enables clinicians to anticipate potential delivery challenges, plan appropriate interventions (such as external cephalic version, cervical assessment, or operative delivery), and optimize outcomes for both mother and child.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: disorders, and obstetric interventions together form a comprehensive framework for risk assessment, care planning, and management of maternal‑fetal outcomes during pregnancy, delivery, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1733
retrieved_docs.content: Temporary and permanent gastrostomy procedures are utilized to provide enteral nutrition (تغذية معوية), allowing direct delivery of nutrients to the stomach for patients who are unable to maintain adequate oral intake.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1966
retrieved_docs.content: Gastrostomy, whether performed as a temporary or permanent procedure, is a key method for providing تغذية معوية (enteral nutrition). Within the broader category of تغذية معوية, التغذية بالأنبوب (tube feeding) represents a more specific, narrower approach that delivers nutrients directly to the gastrointestinal tract. Enteral nutrition is also closely linked to فغر المعدة (gastric ulcer), as nutritional support via gastrostomy or tube feeding is often employed to maintain adequate intake and promote healing in patients affected by gastric ulcer disease. Understanding these interconnections helps clinicians select appropriate feeding strategies and optimize care for individuals requiring enteral nutrition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 121
retrieved_docs.content: formulation design, laboratory evaluation, and patient‑centered nutritional management, and supports clinicians in selecting appropriate supplementation to prevent or treat nutritional deficiencies such as vitamin C deficiency.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: constitute major complications of pregnancy, childbirth, and the puerperium. Recognizing the placement of each specific condition within its broader category supports comprehensive assessment, risk stratification, and coordinated management of pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 189
retrieved_docs.content: each condition as a potential adverse event, enabling systematic risk assessment, targeted monitoring, operative decision‑making, and coordinated maternal‑fetal and newborn care aimed at reducing morbidity and improving outcomes for both mother and infant.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1562
retrieved_docs.content: Assessing or monitoring a patient directly influences the approach to تركيب أنبوب معدي معوي (gastrointestinal tube placement), as careful evaluation and ongoing observation guide the decision‑making, technique selection, and postoperative care associated with this procedure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 130
retrieved_docs.content: cephalopelvic disproportion, cervical pathology, multiple gestation, and drug contraindications—provides a comprehensive framework for obstetric risk assessment, clinical decision‑making, and optimal maternal‑fetal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2502
retrieved_docs.content: Gastrostomy, whether performed as a temporary or permanent procedure, is a method used to deliver enteral nutrition (تغذية معوية). Tube feeding (التغذية بالأنبوب) represents a more specific, narrower form of enteral nutrition. Both gastrostomy and tube feeding are techniques for providing nutrition directly to the gastrointestinal tract, and recognizing their hierarchical relationship assists clinicians in selecting the most appropriate feeding strategy for patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric practice, the overarching topic “Indications for care in pregnancy; labor; and delivery” includes the specific condition “Malposition; malpresentation,” which is classified as a subcategory (has parent) within these care indications. This relationship highlights that malposition and malpresentation are considered part of the broader set of indications guiding clinical management during pregnancy, labor, and delivery.
